summary: DHH recounts a visit to Minneapolis as the final stop on a four-city tour, staying with friends Curtis and Jody. He describes the warm hospitality he received, listing various activities and dining experiences they shared. The post is a brief, affectionate travelogue expressing genuine fondness for Minneapolis and its people. He concludes that he could see himself living there.
key_points: Minneapolis served as the final and most memorable stop on a four-city tour
The hospitality of his hosts Curtis and Jody outshone the natural scenery of Minnesota
The visit featured an eclectic mix of cultural experiences from jazz to state fair food on sticks to diverse cuisines
money_quotes: However impressive the scenery and setting of Minnesota, it paled in light of the royal treatment I enjoyed during my stay.
It was grand. Inviting and delighting. I could easily see myself living in Minneapolis.
key_insight: A personal travelogue celebrating the warmth of Minneapolis hospitality, written years before DHH's public persona took shape.
